summary: A pure AscendC row-copy kernel did not match NPU duplicate-index assignment semantics; switching the binding toward the framework's index_put_ path was required instead of relying on scalar GM writes.

Trying first-occurrence deduplication in the kernel made correctness worse; the fix was to stop approximating duplicate handling in AscendC and use the NPU-side index_put_ implementation in python_bind_src.
Using x.clone() before index_put_ was still slightly different from the reference; aliasing the original tensor handle and then calling index_put_ matched the true inplace_update behavior.
